title: Banke Bihari Temple
tagline: Banke Bihari Temple — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Banke Bihari Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the town of Vrindavan, Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Banke Bihari Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the town of Vrindavan, Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Banke Bihari who is believed to be the combined form of Radha and Krishna. Banke Bihari was originally worshipped at Nidhivan, Vrindavan. Later, when Banke Bihari temple was constructed around 1864, the icon of Banke Bihari was moved to its present temple.

topPlaces.name: Radha Madan Mohan Temple, Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Shri Radha Madan Mohan Temple, also referred as Madan Mohan Temple. is a 16th-century Hindu temple situated in Vrindavan of Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Radha Vallabh Temple, Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Shri Radha Vallabh Temple, also called Shri Radha Vallabhlal ji Temple is a historic temple in the city of Vrindavan, Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Hindu deities Radha Krishna.

topPlaces.name: Radha Damodar Temple, Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Shri Radha Damodar Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Hindu deities Radha and Krishna. The temple is situated in Vrindavan of Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Radha Ramana
topPlaces.description: Radha Ramana (Sanskrit: राधारमण, romanized: rādhāramaṇa) is one of the three combined forms of the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna. The other two forms are Banke Bihari and Radha-Vallabha.

topPlaces.name: Nidhivan, Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Nidhivan (Hindi: निधिवन meaning, Tulsi Forest) is one of the sacred sites of Vrindavan, situated in the Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is considered as the most prominent site dedicated to the pastimes of the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna and their cowherd companions, the Gopikas.

topPlaces.name: Ramakrishna Mission Sevashrama
topPlaces.description: Ramakrishna Mission Sevashrama is a branch of Ramakrishna Math and Ramakrishna Mission involved in providing humanitarian, healthcare, education and relief services to poor and needy through the universal principle of serving "God in man", based on the motto, Atmano Moksartham Jagat Hitaya Cha, which was propounded by Swami Vivekananda.

topPlaces.name: Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Vrindavan (pronounced [ʋɾɪnˈdɑːʋən] ; IAST: Vṛndāvana), also spelt Vrindaban and Brindaban, is a historical city in the Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located in the Braj Bhoomi region and holds religious importance for Hindus, who believe that Krishna, one of the main Gods in Hinduism, spent most of his childhood in this city.

topPlaces.name: Radha Raman Temple, Vrindavan
topPlaces.description: Sri Radha Raman Temple, is a Hindu temple situated in Vrindavan, India. It is dedicated to Krishna who is worshiped as Radha Ramana.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Radha Madan Mohan Temple, Vrindavan & around

items.note: Shri Radha Madan Mohan Temple, also referred as Madan Mohan Temple. is a 16th-century Hindu temple situated in Vrindavan of Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

Shri Radha Vallabh Temple, also called Shri Radha Vallabhlal ji Temple is a historic temple in the city of Vrindavan, Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Hindu deities Radha Krishna.
Shri Radha Damodar Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Hindu deities Radha and Krishna. The temple is situated in Vrindavan of Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.
Radha Ramana (Sanskrit: राधारमण, romanized: rādhāramaṇa) is one of the three combined forms of the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna. The other two forms are Banke Bihari and Radha-Vallabha.

Nidhivan (Hindi: निधिवन meaning, Tulsi Forest) is one of the sacred sites of Vrindavan, situated in the Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is considered as the most prominent site dedicated to the pastimes of the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna and their cowherd companions, the Gopikas.

Ramakrishna Mission Sevashrama is a branch of Ramakrishna Math and Ramakrishna Mission involved in providing humanitarian, healthcare, education and relief services to poor and needy through the universal principle of serving "God in man", based on the motto, Atmano Moksartham Jagat Hitaya Cha, which was propounded by Swami Vivekananda.

Vrindavan (pronounced [ʋɾɪnˈdɑːʋən] ; IAST: Vṛndāvana), also spelt Vrindaban and Brindaban, is a historical city in the Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located in the Braj Bhoomi region and holds religious importance for Hindus, who believe that Krishna, one of the main Gods in Hinduism, spent most of his childhood in this city.
Sri Radha Raman Temple, is a Hindu temple situated in Vrindavan, India. It is dedicated to Krishna who is worshiped as Radha Ramana.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Banke Bihari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Banke Bihari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Banke Bihari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Radha Madan Mohan Temple, Vrindavan, Radha Vallabh Temple, Vrindavan, Radha Damodar Temple, Vrindavan, Radha Ramana, Nidhivan, Vrindavan.
